question: Additive optional aux_models list ({model, training_cutoff?}) on the Arm spec schema: approve the contract change? Additive key, every pre-EVAL-13 spec validates unchanged, no hash-chain migration (the lock hashes bytes); a spec using the key fails loudly on older code via extra='forbid'.
author: claude/design

question: Contamination aggregation for multi-model arms: clean_by_date requires postdating EVERY declared cutoff (effective cutoff = latest declared; dating.py grants clean strictly after a cutoff, so the conservative bound over a set is its maximum), with per-model tri-states listed in the summary — or arm-level aggregate only? NOTE: an earlier draft recommended earliest-cutoff-wins, which is directionally wrong: it would read clean on tasks the newest sub-model could have memorized.
author: claude/design

question: Egress attestation shape and scope. A global host→vendor table is a cheap advisory tripwire but is blind exactly where multi-model arms live: aggregator hosts (OpenRouter/Bedrock/Vertex serve many vendors from one host) and self-hosted endpoints (no a-priori table entry). Per-model declared egress hosts in the spec give real enforcement and fold the currently runtime-config proxy allowlist into pre-registration, at materially more scope. Genuine fork.
author: claude/design

question: Cross-vendor token comparability for a mixed-vendor arm: treat the arm as cross-vendor (token fields marked vendor-incomparable, named in the report; the arm's own token totals additionally labeled mixed-tokenizer in exploratory sections), or exclude the arm's token fields from telemetry sections entirely?
author: claude/design

rationale: Decision session 2026-07-04: per-model egress hosts pre-registered in the spec (arm-level model_hosts keyed by declared model ids) plus experiment-level infra_hosts; the runtime proxy allowlist derives from the locked bytes, closing both the aggregator/self-hosted attribution blind spot and the pre-existing gap that the allowlist was runtime config outside the pre-registration. Residual limit documented: host-level attestation cannot see through an aggregator host multiplexing several models.
migration: additive optional keys; pre-EVAL-13 specs validate unchanged; a spec omitting model_hosts gets attestation coverage only for infra_hosts membership
author: jyang

question: Scope of infra_hosts (package registries, mirrors — the non-model egress allowlist): experiment-level shared by all arms, or per-arm? Shared preserves pairing fairness (both arms face identical infrastructure); per-arm is more flexible but lets infrastructure asymmetry masquerade as a treatment effect.
author: claude/design
